titleStmt.title: Letter from Marie Martin to Mary Martin, 27 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Marie Martin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Marie Martin (1892–1975) to her mother Mary Martin. In the letter, Marie acknowledges extra worries her mother may be experiencing, asking about the health of her brother Laurence John 'Jack' (b. 1899) who is unwell. Marie accounts her good health, except for a stiff shoulder, also that she is relieved to a lightened workload. Mary goes on to thank her mother for a letter she received the day before and hopes her sister Violet is enjoying herself. She writes of the terrible weather conditions she is experiencing.Marie was born in Dublin and trained as a nurse at the outbreak of World War I. Two of Marie's brothers joined the British Army at this time, one of whom was killed in action. Marie served as a nurse in Malta, Leeds and France, and later set up the Medical Missionaries of Mary.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
27 8 16 My very dearest Mother I have been thinking
& wondering a great deal these
last few days how everything is
at home & how Leo is getting
on, it is really too bad you
should have all this extra
worry & it always seems to come
in the Holidaysâ I do hope
Leo is better and all the others
What about Jack is he alright
again, I have been asking about
glands here & they say the
sometimes come from having
adenoids or large tonsils, do you
think Jack has either of these
complaints, he really does not look
strong. Have you been able to
decide what you think best for
him to do. I also hope by this
you will have had some more
good news of Tommyâ I suppose
there is no chance of him getting
home for a bit. I am really keeping A.1 except
for a slight stiff shoulder today
I nave nothing to complain about
I am looking A.1 & my wards
are really quite light now so
get off my schedule time & have
not the same responsibility
which is a great relief, there
are only just the two of us
but the work is not too much
as so many are able to be
up. Really it is very interesting
work, as our M O has made a
special study of Gas poisoning
& he treats them with great
care, it is a rather troublesome
way & take a good deal of timeâ
When they have been up for a
short time he sends them on
a trial walk & then we take a
record of their pulse every min
ute after for about 15 mins & then
he judges if they can walk
down to the Beach once & so on
till at last they can do a long
walk without effecting their
pulsesâ
I have had no more news of
Gerald G. sinceâ but hope all is
well with him. Is there any news
of Eveleen. What splendid news
of Charlie O'Kelly I am so delightâ
ed & as as glad it has all worked
out well for him, I must try &
write to him one of these daysâ
Bernard has not done it yet?
Tell me honestly how are you feeling
these times yourself, I am
longing for your next letter, I
was delighted to get your
card the day before yesterday
to hear Leo is better. I hope
Violet is enjoying herself &
looks better after it & how are
my darlings Desmond & Andrew
I hope none of the others will
get itâ it does seem hard
luck in their holidays always
to be cut off from every one
We have had a most terrible
wet day here toâday & such
a thunder storm, it is beastly
in the tents & so wet & nasty
for the men. I must say good nightâ
With every loving thought, good
wishâ I remain
your most loving & grateful
daughter â Marie
